creator: weh@microsoft.com
text:
Created attachment 202178
Acpidump collected on FreeBSD 12 Guest OS running as a Gen 2 VM on Microsoft Hyper-V

On HyperV Generation 2 VMs, all passthrough devices are attached to pcib0 bus. Upon guest OS boot, pcib0 bus as a device asks to resource 0x2000 byte MMIO memory address space. With the Commit r330133, the request was rejected by nexus_alloc_resource(). Further debugging shows with this commit, the address range requested has been reserved by unknown device much earlier than pcib0‚Äôs request. And almost all good addresses were reserved by this ‚Äòunknown‚Äô device. I put some debug print in the code. Following is the dmesg output:
----
OS boot
‚Ä¶
nexus0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 10, child request range (0xfe0000000-0xfffffffff), count 0x20000000    ÔÉü A request to reserve this amount of memory address in the range.
unknown: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 20, this is the child, needactivate = 0   ÔÉü requesting child is unknown because the name of device_t is empty.
nexus0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, after calling rman_reserve_resource, rv return Not NULL          ÔÉü memory addr successfully reserved for this unknown device
‚Ä¶
nexus0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 10, this is the request bus, range (0xf8000000-0xffdfffff), count 0x7e00000  ÔÉü once again request another chunk of memory address
unknown: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 20, this is the child, needactivate = 0     ÔÉü requesting child is unknown because the name of device_t is empty.
nexus0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, after calling rman_reserve_resource, rv return Not NULL   ÔÉü memory addr successfully reserved again for this unknown device
‚Ä¶
‚Ä¶
nexus0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 10, this is the request bus, range (0xfe0000000-0xfffffffff), count 0x2000   ÔÉü A request to reserve 0x2000 byte in this address range
pcib0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 20, this is the child, needactivate = 2      ÔÉü the requestor is pcib0
vmbus0: ***in pcib_host_res_alloc, after calling bus_generic_alloc_resource, r = NULL    ÔÉü  request failed
‚Ä¶
nexus0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 10, this is the request bus, range (0xf8000000-0xfed3ffff), count 0x2000   ÔÉü Pcib0 tries again in a different address range.
pcib0: &&&in nexus_alloc_resource, check 20, this is the child, needactivate = 2       ÔÉü the requestor is pcib0
vmbus0: ***in pcib_host_res_alloc, after calling bus_generic_alloc_resource, r = NULL    ÔÉü  failed again because both of the address ranges were already reserved by ‚Äòunknown‚Äô device
‚Ä¶
pcib0: failed to get resource for cfg window   ÔÉü picb0 failed to attach, and hence all passthrough devices attached under this bus fail to attach and load.
----

I did a long binary search on head branch and finally narrow down to this commit r330113. The commit was not in 11 branch so passthrough on 11.2 VM still works fine. However 12 release got affected. I suspect this tiny change in ACPI causes the device address interpretation changed. 

Attached is acpidump output I did on a Hyper-V Gen 2 VM with one NIC configured to pass through to the VM.
